Section 1
def count_word_occurrences(text, word):
    normalized_text = text.lower()
    normalized_word = word.lower()
    import re
    pattern = r'\b' + re.escape(normalized_word) + r'\b'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, normalized_text)
    return len(matches)

Section 2
This code handles case sensitivity by converting inputs to lowercase. It uses regular expressions to find whole word matches only. Punctuation is ignored because word boundaries are respected. The function returns the total count found.

Section 3
Testing with the example provided yields the expected result. "Freedom" appears twice in the sample text string. The logic ensures "freedom" matches "Freedom" correctly. No false positives occur from partial matches.
